primary_concern: Sepsis risk with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) consistent with Sepsis-3 criteria. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and hemoglobin of 9.8 g/dL suggest multi-organ involvement. AKI progressed from Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) to Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) within 24 hours. Platelets remain below 100 K/uL across three measurements, meeting SOFA coagulation criteria.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine rose 6.5 mg/dL in 6 hours (excluded outlier) and 2.1 mg/dL over 24 hours
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7 days

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL for 36+ hours with progressive decline
prioritized_risks.threshold: Platelets <150 K/uL triggers SOFA coagulation scoring

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hypotension and vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection source workup
Start fluid resuscitation and reassess for vasopressor requirements
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion threshold evaluation
Review excluded creatinine outlier for specimen integrity
doctor_handoff: Verify infection source and sepsis response completion. Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen accuracy. Assess for new bleeding risks with persistent thrombocytopenia.

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
